name: Corn, Shrimp and Feta Salad with Chili-Lime Vinaigrette
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons fresh lime juice, or more to taste
1/2 teaspoon salt, or more to taste
1/4 teaspoon freshly ground pepper, or more to taste
1 pound large shrimp, peeled and deveined
4 ears corn, with husks and silks
3 ripe plum tomatoes, seeded and cut into 1/2-inch cubes
2 scallions, white and green parts trimmed and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the dressing: In a medium bowl, whisk together the lime juice, chili powder, oregano, garlic, salt, and pepper. Gradually whisk in the olive oil.
2. Assemble the salad: Toss the shrimp with 3 tablespoons of the dressing. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to up to 2 hours. Refrigerate the remaining dressing separately.
3. Meanwhile, build a hot charcoal fire in an outdoor grill or preheat a gas grill on high. Lightly oil the grill. Place the corn and shrimp on the grill.
4. Grill the shrimp until pink and firm, about 5 minutes. Cover and refrigerate the shrimp until ready to use. Continue grilling the corn, turning occasionally, until the husks are scorched all over, about 12 more minutes.
5. Cool the corn until it is easy to handle. Remove the husks and silk. Using a sharp knife, cut off the end of an ear of corn. Hold the corn firmly on the end and cut down the cob, cutting away the kernels. Repeat with the remaining ears. You should have about 2 cups of kernels.
6. Cut each shrimp lengthwise into halves. In a large serving bowl, toss the shrimp, corn, tomatoes, scallions, and jalapeno with the remaining dressing. Cover and refrigerate until chilled, about 2 hours. Taste and adjust the seasoning with lime juice, salt, and pepper as needed. Sprinkle with feta cheese and serve.
